originalTitle: The Guardian’s Kai Wright refuses to buy a new phone
summary: Kai Wright is the co-host of Stateside with Kai and Carter over at the Guardian. But Wright has been bringing his unique insights to listeners for years. He's also hosted Notes From America, The United States of Anxiety, and Indivisible. He's a Peabody Award-winning journalist who has profiled powerful men, explored what it means to […]
content: Kai Wright is the co-host of Stateside with Kai and Carter over at the Guardian. But Wright has been bringing his unique insights to listeners for years. He’s also hosted Notes From America, The United States of Anxiety, and Indivisible. He’s a Peabody Award-winning journalist who has profiled powerful men, explored what it means to be American, and chronicled the AIDS epidemic. When he’s not diving deep on sex, race, and politics, he’s gardening, listening to John Coltrane, and steadfastly refusing to buy a new phone. Wright was kind enough to give us a glimpse into his daily routine, how he unwinds, and offer some simple but powerful advice. What is your most indispensable tool? A wine key. Which is the most underappreciated? Wheelbarrow. Seriously, though. Haul around enough dirt and rocks, and you will come to appreciate the brilliant utility of this basic design. What is one thing you wish you could change about your phone? I wish I could have bought one smartphone and never ever had to buy another one again, forever. Why must it constantly go obsolete? (Of course, I haven’t actually bought a new phone in I dunno how many years; I just take my partner’s hand-me-downs. So I’m complaining on cranky principle.) How many tabs do you have open right now? In the window I’m using right this moment, I have 10 tabs open. Yes, this is quite a reasonable number, thank you. I do have 11 more windows open, however, and cannot bring myself to account for what’s in them. Which was the most disappointing gadget you’ve ever owned? I won’t name brands, but I promise you can make a coffee machine too complicated. I’m trying to get caffeinated so I can become a functioning human. I don’t want an engineering challenge.  What is one thing you wish you had created? I wish I had thought of The Memory Palace - which is still, to me, podcasting at its best. Short, sweet, deeply human histories of our world. One person, telling you a well-written story. What creation are you most proud of? Can I say my whole, entire garden? It’s an exercise in constantly learning and applying the knowledge and trying again, and I hear from veteran gardeners that you never actually figure it out. Perfect.  What’s the best piece of advice you’ve ever received? Live an honest life. Meaning, try your best to tell yourself the truth, and make choices accordingly. What do you do when you’re feeling stuck? Listen to John Coltrane records. What’s the last piece of physical media you bought? A book about gardening. What would the tagline for your biopic be? “What had happened was…” What’s the last GIF or meme you used? [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/image1.gif?quality=90&strip=all]
chineseTitle: 《卫报》的凯·赖特拒绝购买新手机
chineseSummary: 凯·赖特 (Kai Wright) 是《美国本土》节目的联合主持人，凯·赖特 (Kai Wright) 和卡特 (Carter) 是《卫报》的主持人。但赖特多年来一直为听众带来他独特的见解。他还主持过《美国笔记》、《焦虑的美国》和《不可分割》。他是一位获得皮博迪奖的记者，曾报道过有权有势的人物，探索过 […]
chineseContent: 凯·赖特 (Kai Wright) 是《美国本土》节目的联合主持人，凯·赖特 (Kai Wright) 和卡特 (Carter) 是《卫报》的主持人。但赖特多年来一直为听众带来他独特的见解。他还主持过《美国笔记》、《焦虑的美国》和《不可分割》。他是一位获得皮博迪奖的记者，曾报道过有权有势的人物，探讨了身为美国人的意义，并记录了艾滋病的流行。当他不深入研究性、种族和政治时，他会做园艺、听约翰·科尔特兰 (John Coltrane) 的演讲，并坚决拒绝购买新手机。赖特很友善地让我们了解了他的日常生活、他如何放松，并提供了一些简单但有力的建议。你最不可或缺的工具是什么？酒钥匙。哪个最被低估？独轮车。不过说真的。拖运足够多的泥土和岩石，您就会欣赏到这种基本设计的出色实用性。您希望可以改变手机的哪一件事？我希望我可以购买一部智能手机，并且永远不必再购买另一部智能手机。为什么它必须不断过时？ （当然，我不知道多少年没有买过新手机了；我只是拿了我伴侣的旧衣服。所以我是在抱怨胡思乱想。）你现在打开了多少个标签？在我现在使用的窗口中，我打开了 10 个选项卡。是的，这是一个相当合理的数字，谢谢。然而，我确实还打开了 11 个窗口，无法解释其中的内容。您拥有过的最令人失望的小玩意是什么？我不会说出品牌的名字，但我保证你可以把咖啡机做得太复杂。我正在努力摄入咖啡因，这样我就可以成为一个正常的人。我不想要工程挑战。  您希望自己创造的一件事是什么？我希望我有你
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
